title: Implement 40-bit OSCORE sequences
description: Rust OSCORE stores sender/replay sequence state as u32 even though RFC 8613 permits 40-bit Partial IVs. Five-byte PIVs above u32::MAX saturate/collapse, breaking replay ordering and interoperability. Convert sequence representation and vector consumers to bounded u64 (0..2^40-1), reject overlong/out-of-range values, and preserve terminal exhaustion without mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Literal boundaries 0xff,0x100,u32::MAX,u32::MAX+1,2^40-1 roundtrip; 2^40 and >5-byte PIV reject; five-byte replay ordering and duplicates work; request/response terminal exhaustion never wraps; debug/release/no_std/all-feature tests, Clippy, and three reviews pass.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with RFC-literal tests; debug/release/all-feature/no-default suites and library Clippy pass; three hash-bound reviews found no remaining in-scope P0-P2 defects. Context lifecycle remains .48.2.
